channel.title: fish-shellfish-plates posts - RSS Feed
channel.description: Fresh fish-shellfish-plates posts and cooking inspiration

item.title: Fast and Flavorful Grilled Salmon with Spring Herb Sauce That Will Wow Your Taste Buds
item.description: This grilled salmon is quick, packed with fresh spring herbs, and bursting with flavor. The vibrant herb sauce brightens the dish perfectly. Ideal for a light dinner or weekend cookout!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 15 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 salmon fillets, skin on, about 6 oz each
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil, divided
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
  • 1 cup fresh parsley leaves, packed
  • 1/2 cup fresh basil leaves
  • 1/4 cup fresh mint leaves
  • 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
  • 1 garlic clove, minced
  • 1/3 cup extra virgin olive oil
  • Zest of 1 lemon

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by prepping your salmon fillets. Pat them dry with paper towels — this helps the skin crisp up nicely on the grill. Brush both sides lightly with olive oil and season generously with salt and pepper. This step is crucial because dry skin ensures it won’t stick to the grill, and seasoning enhances the natural flavors. Avoid rushing this part; take your time so the seasoning absorbs well. 5 minutes Salmon skin feels dry to the touch and fillets are evenly coated with oil and seasoning.
  2. 2 Preheat your grill to medium-high heat, around 400°F. Place the salmon skin-side down and grill without moving for about 6-7 minutes. This allows the skin to crisp and the fish to cook evenly. Resist the urge to flip too soon; if it sticks, it’s not ready. When the skin releases easily, flip the fillets and cook for another 3-4 minutes until just cooked through. 10-12 minutes Salmon is opaque and flakes easily with a fork, skin is crisp and golden brown.
  3. 3 While the salmon cooks, prepare the spring herb sauce. In a food processor or blender, combine parsley, basil, mint, lemon juice, garlic, lemon zest, and olive oil. Pulse until smooth but still a bit textured. This fresh sauce adds brightness and balances the richness of the salmon perfectly. Make sure not to over-blend, or it becomes too watery. 5 minutes Sauce is vibrant green with a slightly chunky texture and fresh aroma.
  4. 4 Once the salmon is done, transfer to a serving plate. Spoon the herb sauce generously over each fillet while still warm. The warmth helps release the sauce’s aroma and melds the flavors beautifully. Serve immediately with your favorite sides like grilled veggies or a simple salad. 2 minutes Salmon glistens with sauce, and the aroma of fresh herbs fills the air.
  5. 5 For a perfect finish, squeeze a little extra lemon juice over the plated salmon just before serving. This adds a fresh zing and balances the richness. It’s a small step but really makes a difference in brightening the whole dish. 1 minute A light citrus aroma and a subtle tang on the palate.
  6. 6 If you don’t have a grill, you can use a grill pan or broiler. Just make sure to preheat it well and watch the salmon closely to avoid overcooking. The key is getting that nice char without drying out the fish. Variable Char marks visible and salmon flakes easily.
  7. 7 Rest the salmon for a few minutes before serving if you’re not plating immediately. This lets the juices redistribute and keeps the fish moist. Cover loosely with foil to retain warmth. 3-5 minutes Salmon feels warm but not steaming hot, moist texture.
  8. 8 Taste the herb sauce before serving and adjust seasoning if needed. Sometimes a pinch of salt or a splash more lemon juice can make it pop. Fresh herbs can vary in intensity, so trust your palate. 1-2 minutes Balanced flavor with fresh, bright notes.
  9. 9 Serve the grilled salmon with your favorite sides—roasted potatoes, steamed asparagus, or a crisp green salad all work beautifully. The herb sauce complements a variety of flavors, so feel free to get creative. Enjoy! 0 minutes A complete, colorful plate ready to enjoy.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761230072178 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #salmon #olive #freshly

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: This grilled salmon is quick, packed with fresh spring herbs, and bursting with flavor. The vibrant herb sauce brightens the dish perfectly. Ideal for a light dinner or weekend cookout!

item.title: Easy Baked Lemon Dill Salmon With Asparagus And Peas That’s Ready in 30 Minutes
item.description: This baked salmon dish is light, fresh, and perfect for a quick weeknight dinner. The lemon and dill brighten the fish while tender asparagus and sweet peas round out the meal. It’s an effortless, healthy plate you’ll want to make again.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 20 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 salmon fillets, skin on, about 6 oz each
  • 1 bunch asparagus, trimmed
  • 1 cup fresh or frozen peas
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
  • 1 tablespoon fresh dill, chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
  • Lemon slices, for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or lightly grease it to prevent sticking. This step ensures even cooking and prevents the salmon from sticking, which can tear the delicate flesh when you try to remove it later. Using parchment also helps with cleanup, especially since the veggies will roast alongside the fish. 10 minutes Oven fully preheated and baking sheet prepared
  2. 2 Place the salmon fillets skin-side down on the baking sheet. Drizzle each fillet with 1 tablespoon olive oil, then sprinkle with salt, pepper, minced garlic, and chopped fresh dill. Finish by squeezing fresh lemon juice over the top. This layering of flavors helps the salmon stay moist and infuses it with bright citrus notes. The oil also aids in browning and prevents drying out during baking. 5 minutes Salmon evenly coated and seasoned, lemon juice visibly fresh
  3. 3 Arrange the trimmed asparagus and peas around the salmon on the baking sheet. Drizzle the vegetables with the remaining olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Toss gently to coat but keep them in one layer for even roasting. Cooking the veggies with the fish saves time and lets their natural sweetness develop alongside the salmon’s rich flavor. 5 minutes Vegetables evenly coated and spread out in a single layer
  4. 4 Place the baking sheet in the preheated oven and bake for 15 to 20 minutes. The salmon should be opaque and flake easily with a fork, while the asparagus becomes tender but still crisp. Avoid overcooking to keep the fish juicy and the veggies vibrant. Check at 15 minutes to gauge doneness and adjust time as needed based on your oven and fillet thickness. 15-20 minutes Salmon flakes easily and veggies are tender-crisp
  5. 5 Remove the baking sheet from the oven and let the salmon rest for 3-5 minutes before serving. Resting allows the juices to redistribute through the fish, ensuring each bite is moist and flavorful. This small step makes a big difference in texture and taste. 3-5 minutes Salmon slightly firm but still moist, juices settled
  6. 6 Garnish the salmon with fresh lemon slices and an extra sprinkle of dill if desired. This final touch adds a pop of color and a fresh aroma that enhances the dish’s appeal. Plus, it lets people add more lemon flavor to their liking. 2 minutes Dish visually bright with lemon slices and herbs
  7. 7 Serve the salmon with the roasted asparagus and peas immediately while warm. The combination of tender fish and crisp-tender veggies makes for a balanced, satisfying meal. Pair with a simple side like quinoa or crusty bread if you want to stretch it further. Immediate Dish plated and ready to enjoy
  8. 8 If you want to add a little extra richness, drizzle a teaspoon of good quality olive oil or a small pat of butter over the salmon just before serving. This adds a silky finish that complements the lemon and dill beautifully. Optional Salmon glistening with added fat
  9. 9 Clean up by tossing the parchment paper and wiping down the baking sheet. Since everything cooks on one sheet, cleanup is quick and easy, which means less time in the kitchen and more time enjoying your meal. 5 minutes Kitchen tidy and ready for next cooking adventure

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761230072178 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #salmon #bunch #fresh

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Easy Baked Lemon Dill Salmon With Asparagus And Peas That’s Ready in 30 Minutes
media:content.media:description: This baked salmon dish is light, fresh, and perfect for a quick weeknight dinner. The lemon and dill brighten the fish while tender asparagus and sweet peas round out the meal. It’s an effortless, healthy plate you’ll want to make again.

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761230072178, Easypost, 1hourmeals, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, salmon, bunch, fresh

item.title: Easy 30-Minute Creamy Garlic Butter Salmon With Asparagus
item.description: This salmon dish is a quick, creamy delight that combines tender fish with garlicky butter sauce and crisp asparagus. The secret? A splash of cream that makes the sauce irresistibly smooth. Ideal for weeknight dinners when you want something fancy but fast!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 salmon fillets, skin on, about 6 oz each
  • 1 bunch asparagus, trimmed
  • 3 tablespoons unsalted butter
  • 4 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1/2 cup heavy cream
  • 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
  • 1 teaspoon lemon juice
  • Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Start by prepping your salmon fillets and asparagus. Pat the salmon dry with paper towels to help achieve a nice sear. Trim the woody ends off the asparagus and set aside. Dry salmon skin helps it crisp up during cooking, which adds great texture and flavor. Avoid skipping this step because moisture on the fish will steam it instead of searing, resulting in less appealing texture. 5 minutes Salmon surface feels dry and firm to touch before cooking
  2. 2 Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Place the salmon fillets skin-side down and cook without moving them for about 5-6 minutes. This allows the skin to crisp up beautifully and the fish to cook most of the way through. If you flip too early, the skin might stick and tear. Patience here is key for that perfect crust. 6 minutes Skin is golden brown and releases easily from the pan
  3. 3 Flip the salmon carefully and add the trimmed asparagus to the skillet. Cook for another 3-4 minutes, turning asparagus occasionally so it gets tender but still crisp. Cooking the asparagus in the same pan lets it soak up salmon flavors and butter later on. Overcooking will make it mushy, so keep an eye on the texture. 4 minutes Salmon is opaque and asparagus is bright green and tender-crisp
  4. 4 Reduce the heat to medium-low and add butter to the skillet. Once melted, stir in minced garlic. Cooking garlic gently in butter releases its aroma without burning it, which would make it bitter. Stir constantly and watch closely to keep the garlic golden and fragrant. 2 minutes Garlic is fragrant and lightly golden
  5. 5 Pour in the heavy cream and stir to combine with the butter and garlic. Let it simmer gently for 2-3 minutes until it slightly thickens. The cream enriches the sauce, making it luscious and smooth. Avoid boiling hard, which can cause the cream to separate and curdle. 3 minutes Sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon
  6. 6 Add the grated Parmesan cheese and stir until melted and fully incorporated. The cheese adds a nutty depth and helps thicken the sauce further. Use freshly grated Parmesan for best texture and flavor; pre-grated can be powdery and less flavorful. 2 minutes Sauce is creamy and smooth with no lumps
  7. 7 Season the sauce with salt, freshly ground black pepper, and a splash of lemon juice to brighten the flavors. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Lemon juice cuts through the richness and balances the dish beautifully. 1 minute Sauce tastes balanced with a hint of brightness
  8. 8 Spoon the creamy garlic butter sauce over the salmon and asparagus in the skillet. Let everything warm together for a minute so the flavors meld. This final step ensures the salmon stays moist and the asparagus is perfectly coated. 1 minute Sauce evenly coats salmon and asparagus, glossy appearance
  9. 9 Garnish with freshly chopped parsley before serving. The fresh herb adds a pop of color and a subtle freshness that complements the richness of the dish. Serve immediately with your favorite side or a simple salad. 1 minute Bright green parsley scattered over the dish

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761230072178 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #salmon #bunch #unsalted

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Easy 30-Minute Creamy Garlic Butter Salmon With Asparagus
media:content.media:description: This salmon dish is a quick, creamy delight that combines tender fish with garlicky butter sauce and crisp asparagus. The secret? A splash of cream that makes the sauce irresistibly smooth. Ideal for weeknight dinners when you want something fancy but fast!

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761230072178, Easypost, 1hourmeals, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, salmon, bunch, unsalted

item.title: Quick 25-Minute Baked Cod with Spring Vegetable Medley That’s Fresh and Light
item.description: This baked cod dish is a fresh, light dinner that comes together in just 25 minutes. The spring vegetables add a crisp, colorful touch and a subtle sweetness that pairs beautifully with flaky cod. Perfect for a quick weeknight meal that feels special!
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 10 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 15 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 cod fillets, about 6 oz each, skinless and boneless
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil, plus extra for drizzling
  • 1 lemon, zested and juiced
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 teaspoon dried thyme
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
  • 1 cup asparagus, trimmed and cut into 2-inch pieces
  • 1 cup snap peas, trimmed
  • 1 cup baby carrots, halved lengthwise
  • 1/2 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
  • 2 tablespoons fresh parsley, chopped

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). While it heats, prepare the vegetables by trimming and chopping the asparagus, snap peas, baby carrots, and halving the cherry tomatoes. Toss them in a large bowl with half the olive oil, salt, and pepper. This step ensures the veggies get evenly coated and will roast nicely, developing a natural sweetness and slight caramelization that adds depth to the dish. 10 minutes Vegetables are evenly coated with oil and seasoning, ready for roasting.
  2. 2 Place the cod fillets on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a lightly greased baking dish. Drizzle with the remaining olive oil, then sprinkle with garlic, lemon zest, thyme, salt, and pepper. The lemon zest adds a fresh citrus aroma that infuses the fish as it bakes. Seasoning the fish properly before baking is key to enhancing its natural mild flavor without overpowering it. 5 minutes Fillets are evenly seasoned and look glossy from the olive oil.
  3. 3 Arrange the seasoned vegetables around the cod fillets on the baking sheet, spreading them out to ensure even roasting. This arrangement allows the fish and veggies to cook simultaneously, saving time and letting the flavors mingle. Avoid overcrowding to prevent steaming instead of roasting, which would make the veggies soggy. 3 minutes Vegetables are spread out in a single layer around the fish.
  4. 4 Place the baking sheet in the preheated oven and roast for 12-15 minutes. The fish should be opaque and flake easily with a fork, while the vegetables become tender but still retain a slight crunch. Keep an eye on the cherry tomatoes—they should soften but not burst excessively, adding a juicy contrast to the dish. 12-15 minutes Cod is opaque and flakes easily; vegetables are tender-crisp.
  5. 5 Remove the baking sheet from the oven and squeeze fresh lemon juice over the cod and vegetables. This final touch adds brightness and balances the richness of the olive oil. Toss the vegetables gently to coat them in the lemon juice and any pan juices from the fish for extra flavor. 2 minutes Dish is glistening with fresh lemon juice, aromas are vibrant.
  6. 6 Sprinkle chopped fresh parsley over the entire dish for a burst of color and a fresh herbal note. This also adds a slight peppery flavor that complements the lemon and garlic beautifully. Serve immediately while warm for the best texture and flavor. 1 minute Parsley is evenly distributed, dish looks vibrant and inviting.
  7. 7 Optional: If you like a little extra texture, sprinkle some toasted pine nuts or slivered almonds on top before serving. This adds a nice crunch and subtle nuttiness that pairs well with the mild cod and fresh veggies. 2 minutes Nuts are lightly toasted and evenly sprinkled over the dish.
  8. 8 Pair this meal with a simple green salad or crusty bread to soak up any juices. The combination makes for a well-rounded, satisfying dinner that feels light but still filling. The balance of textures and flavors makes this a dish you’ll want to make again. Optional serving prep Sides are prepared and complement the main dish.
  9. 9 Enjoy your meal fresh from the oven! Leftovers can be stored in the fridge but are best eaten within a day or two to maintain the cod’s delicate texture and the veggies’ crispness. N/A Dish is served warm and fresh.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761230072178 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #fillets #tablespoon #lemon

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Quick 25-Minute Baked Cod with Spring Vegetable Medley That’s Fresh and Light
media:content.media:description: This baked cod dish is a fresh, light dinner that comes together in just 25 minutes. The spring vegetables add a crisp, colorful touch and a subtle sweetness that pairs beautifully with flaky cod. Perfect for a quick weeknight meal that feels special!

item.title: Easy One-Pan Baked Salmon with Fresh Spring Pea Puree
item.description: This one-pan baked salmon is a breeze to make and feels so fresh with a bright spring pea puree. I love how the puree adds a creamy, vibrant touch without any fuss. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner or a light weekend meal.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 salmon fillets, skin on, about 6 oz each
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
  • 1 cup fresh or frozen peas
  • 1/4 cup heavy cream or crème fraîche
  • 1 small garlic clove, minced
  • 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice
  • 1 tablespoon fresh mint leaves, chopped
  • Lemon wedges, for serving

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat the salmon fillets dry with paper towels to ensure the skin crisps up nicely during baking. Drizzle olive oil over the fillets and season both sides generously with salt and pepper. This step is crucial because dry skin crisps better and seasoning builds flavor right from the start. Avoid skipping the drying step as moisture will steam the skin instead of crisping it. 10 minutes Salmon skin looks dry and slightly shiny from the oil, with even seasoning coverage.
  2. 2 Place the salmon fillets skin-side down on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a lightly oiled pan. Bake in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes, depending on thickness. You want the salmon to be just cooked through and still moist inside. Overbaking dries it out, so check for flakiness with a fork near the end. The skin should be crisp and the flesh opaque but tender. 12-15 minutes Flesh turns opaque and flakes easily when prodded gently with a fork.
  3. 3 While the salmon bakes, bring a small pot of water to a boil and add the peas. Cook for 2-3 minutes until tender but still bright green. Overcooking will dull the color and reduce sweetness, so watch carefully. Drain the peas and immediately transfer them to a bowl of ice water to stop cooking and preserve that vibrant hue. 5 minutes Peas are bright green and tender but not mushy.
  4. 4 In a blender or food processor, combine the cooked peas, minced garlic, heavy cream, lemon juice, and chopped mint. Blend until smooth and creamy. The cream adds richness while lemon brightens the flavor. Mint gives a fresh herbal note that complements the peas beautifully. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. The puree should be smooth and vibrant. 5 minutes Puree is smooth, creamy, and bright green with a fresh aroma.
  5. 5 To serve, spoon a generous amount of pea puree onto each plate and top with a baked salmon fillet. Garnish with lemon wedges for squeezing over the fish. The contrast of the creamy puree and flaky salmon makes every bite delightful. This plating also keeps the dish visually fresh and inviting. 5 minutes Plates look colorful and appetizing with vibrant green puree and golden salmon.
  6. 6 Optional: If you want extra crispiness, once the salmon is baked, place it under the broiler for 1-2 minutes watching closely. This step enhances the skin texture but be careful not to burn. It’s a nice touch if you like a crunchier finish. 1-2 minutes Skin bubbles and crisps without charring.
  7. 7 If you prefer a lighter puree, swap heavy cream for a splash of olive oil or vegetable broth. This will make the puree less rich but still flavorful. Adjust seasoning accordingly. It’s a great tweak for a dairy-free option. 2 minutes Puree remains smooth but slightly thinner in texture.
  8. 8 For an extra flavor boost, stir in a teaspoon of Dijon mustard or a pinch of chili flakes into the pea puree. Mustard adds tanginess while chili flakes bring a subtle heat. Both elevate the dish without overpowering the delicate salmon. 2 minutes Puree has a slight spicy or tangy aroma depending on addition.
  9. 9 Let the salmon rest for a couple of minutes after baking before serving. This helps juices redistribute and keeps the fish moist. Cutting immediately can cause the juices to run out and make the salmon dry. 3 minutes Salmon feels slightly firm but still moist when pressed gently.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761230072178 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #salmon #tablespoon #freshly

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Easy One-Pan Baked Salmon with Fresh Spring Pea Puree
media:content.media:description: This one-pan baked salmon is a breeze to make and feels so fresh with a bright spring pea puree. I love how the puree adds a creamy, vibrant touch without any fuss. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner or a light weekend meal.

item.title: Quick 25-Minute Baked Salmon with Fresh Spring Pea Puree
item.description: This baked salmon is a weeknight winner—juicy, tender, and paired with a bright spring pea puree that adds a fresh pop. The puree’s vibrant color and subtle sweetness make it perfect for spring dinners or any time you want something light but satisfying.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 10 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 15 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 salmon fillets, skin on, about 6 oz each
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1 cup fresh or frozen spring peas
  • 1/4 cup fresh mint leaves
  • 2 tablespoons lemon juice
  • 2 tablespoons heavy cream
  • 1 small garlic clove, minced
  • Salt to taste for puree

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat the salmon fillets dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture; this helps the skin crisp up nicely during baking. Lightly brush both sides of the salmon with olive oil and season generously with salt and pepper. Placing the salmon skin-side down on a parchment-lined baking sheet helps prevent sticking and promotes even cooking. Avoid overcrowding the pan so the heat circulates properly around each fillet. 10 minutes Salmon fillets have a slightly golden crust on top, and the skin is crisp to the touch.
  2. 2 Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, depending on thickness. The goal is to cook it until it flakes easily with a fork but remains moist inside. Avoid overbaking, which dries out the fish. Start checking at 12 minutes by gently pressing the thickest part; it should feel firm but still give slightly. If you have a thermometer, aim for an internal temperature of 125-130°F for medium doneness. 12-15 minutes Salmon flakes easily when tested with a fork and has an opaque pink color inside.
  3. 3 While the salmon bakes, bring a small pot of salted water to a boil. Add the spring peas and cook just until tender, about 2-3 minutes. Drain immediately and transfer the peas to a blender or food processor. This quick cooking preserves their bright color and fresh flavor. 5 minutes Peas are bright green and tender but not mushy.
  4. 4 Add the fresh mint leaves, lemon juice, minced garlic, and heavy cream to the peas in the blender. Blend until smooth and creamy. Season with salt to taste. The cream adds richness and smooth texture, balancing the brightness of the peas and lemon. 3 minutes Puree is smooth, vibrant green, and has a balanced tangy and fresh aroma.
  5. 5 Taste the pea puree and adjust seasoning if needed, adding more lemon juice or salt to brighten or enhance flavor. If it’s too thick, add a splash of water or more cream to loosen it up. Set aside and keep warm. 2 minutes Puree has a smooth, spreadable consistency and fresh, bright taste.
  6. 6 Once salmon is done, remove from the oven and let it rest for a couple of minutes. Resting allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the fish moist and flavorful when you cut into it. 5 minutes Salmon retains moisture and flakes gently without falling apart.
  7. 7 To serve, spoon a generous amount of the spring pea puree onto each plate. Place the salmon fillet on top, skin-side down. The puree acts as a fresh bed that complements the richness of the salmon beautifully. 2 minutes Plated dish looks vibrant with a nice contrast of colors and textures.
  8. 8 Garnish with a few fresh mint leaves or a wedge of lemon if you like. The garnish adds a little visual flair and a hint of extra freshness. 1 minute Dish looks inviting and fresh with bright green and golden hues.
  9. 9 Enjoy immediately while the salmon is warm and the puree creamy. This dish pairs well with a simple green salad or roasted potatoes for a complete meal. 0 minutes Salmon is warm, flaky, and the puree is fresh and creamy.

📌 Pinterest Tags: #cat1761230072178 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #salmon #freshly #tablespoon

media:content.media:title: Quick 25-Minute Baked Salmon with Fresh Spring Pea Puree
media:content.media:description: This baked salmon is a weeknight winner—juicy, tender, and paired with a bright spring pea puree that adds a fresh pop. The puree’s vibrant color and subtle sweetness make it perfect for spring dinners or any time you want something light but satisfying.

item.title: Pan-Seared Shrimp with Garlic and Lemon: Technique and Timing
item.description: A straightforward guide to cooking shrimp perfectly every time. Learn why proper pan searing requires dry shrimp, high heat, and minimal crowding, plus the exact doneness cues that prevent overcooking this delicate protein.
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 10 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 25 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1.5 lbs (680g) large shrimp (16-20 count per pound), peeled and deveined
  • 3 tbsp (45ml) extra-virgin olive oil, divided
  • 4 cloves (20g) garlic, thinly sliced
  • 1/2 lemon (25g), cut into wedges
  • 1/2 tsp (3g) fine sea salt
  • 1/4 tsp (0.5g) black pepper
  • 1/4 tsp (0.5g) red pepper flakes (optional)
  • 2 tbsp (30g) unsalted butter (optional, for finishing)
  • 2 tbsp (8g) fresh parsley, chopped (optional garnish)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Remove the shrimp from the refrigerator 10-12 minutes before cooking. Cold shrimp coming straight from the refrigerator will dramatically cool down your pan when added, dropping the surface temperature by 50-100°F or more. This temperature drop is the enemy of good browning. As the shrimp sits at room temperature, the interior gradually warms while the exterior reaches thermal equilibrium with the air. You'll notice the shrimp become more flexible and pliable as they warm. If your kitchen is cold, give them the full 12 minutes. If it's warm, 10 minutes is sufficient. Check that your shrimp are indeed deveined by running your finger along the back curve—you should feel a clean line without any dark vein running along the back. If you see any remaining dark matter, use a small pointed knife to trace the vein and lift it out. A small amount of residual vein won't harm the final dish, but removing it creates cleaner appearance and slightly better texture. 10-12 minutes Shrimp feel cool but not cold to touch, flesh is flexible and pliable, no condensation visible on surface
  2. 2 Pat the shrimp completely dry using paper towels. This step determines whether your sear will succeed or fail, yet it's the step most people rush. Press gently but firmly on each shrimp's surface, rotating it to dry all sides, especially the underside which tends to hold moisture. This isn't a light dab—this is actual pressure, using multiple paper towels if needed. You'll feel moisture transfer to the towel. Once you think they're dry, do another pass. I typically use three passes of paper towels for a pound and a half of shrimp. Hold a shrimp up to the light and look at its surface—it should appear completely matte, not shiny. Any glistening indicates residual moisture. If moisture remains when the shrimp hits the hot oil, it will create steam that prevents the shrimp from reaching the pan's surface temperature. This forces you to cook longer to compensate, inevitably overcooking the interior. 5-7 minutes Shrimp surface appears completely matte with no shine, no moisture transfers to towel when pressed, shrimp doesn't feel slippery when handled
  3. 3 Place a large skillet (10-12 inches / 25-30 cm) over medium-high heat and add 2 tablespoons (30ml) of extra-virgin olive oil. The pan should be completely dry—any water remaining from washing will cause the oil to splatter when it heats. Wait about 2 minutes before adding shrimp. You'll see the oil's surface become fluid and start to shimmer. When you tilt the pan, the oil should ripple across the entire surface. The oil should not smoke—if it does, your heat is too high and the oil's chemical structure is breaking down, which degrades flavor and creates off-tastes. Medium-high heat is the correct setting. If your stovetop runs hot, you might need to set it between medium and medium-high. The goal is an audible sizzle that's steady and purposeful, not violent or aggressive. 2 minutes Oil shimmers across entire pan surface, small heat waves visible above oil, no smoke present, oil flows freely when pan is tilted
  4. 4 Working in two batches if necessary, arrange shrimp in the hot oil in a single uncrowded layer. Space them so there's approximately a quarter-inch (6mm) of visible pan surface between each shrimp. They should not touch. This spacing is non-negotiable. The space between shrimp allows air circulation and ensures heat reaches all surfaces evenly. More importantly, each shrimp maintains contact with the hot pan surface, not with other shrimp. In crowded pans, shrimp touch each other and essentially steam where they contact. If your pan typically holds eight shrimp comfortably spaced, cook eight at a time. Resist the urge to fit more—it's far better to cook three batches of eight shrimp than to crowd the pan and end up with steamed, pale shrimp. Do not move or flip the shrimp for the first 2 minutes. Resist the urge to check on them. The browning (Maillard reaction) develops on the bottom surface only through sustained direct contact with the hot pan. Moving the shrimp breaks this contact and restarts the process. You should hear a steady, purposeful sizzle. If it's a soft hiss, the pan isn't hot enough. If it's a violent pop, the heat is slightly too high and the oil may be starting to smoke. 2 minutes (first side) Steady audible sizzle, shrimp bottoms develop golden-brown tint visible along edges, shrimp edges begin to curl slightly at the very ends, immediate aroma of searing shrimp and garlic becomes apparent
  5. 5 Flip each shrimp and cook for exactly 1.5 to 2 minutes on the second side. This is the moment where precision matters most. Look at the shrimp's color—it should be opaque and white across approximately 90% of its surface. The very center of the thickest part may still show a barely-visible thin line of slight translucence when you look at the shrimp straight-on. This translucence indicates the interior is still at 140-145°F (60-63°C)—exactly where you want it. If you see the shrimp forming a tight C-shape with the edges curled severely, you've exceeded the target temperature. The proteins have contracted so much that the shrimp is now tough. The bottom side will brown less than the first side because the pan has lost some heat and the exterior is already setting, but this is normal and actually desirable. A shrimp with one golden side and one slightly lighter side indicates perfect timing. Uniform browning on both sides typically indicates slight overcooking. 1.5 to 2 minutes (second side) Shrimp mostly opaque white with opaque center, barely-visible translucence only at the absolute middle when viewed straight-on, sides beginning to curl but not extreme, internal temperature reaches 140-145°F (60-63°C) if measured with an instant-read thermometer
  6. 6 Add the sliced garlic and red pepper flakes (if using) to the pan and stir constantly for 20-30 seconds. This is the only moment when raw garlic goes into the pan, and the timing is critical. If you add garlic at the beginning of cooking alongside the shrimp, the sustained high heat will burn it, creating bitter, acrid flavors. By adding garlic only now, after the shrimp finishes cooking, you're relying on the pan's residual heat—still very hot, but dropping gradually. The garlic's sulfur compounds break down and become mellow and sweet rather than sharp and pungent. The garlic won't color or brown visibly; it will remain pale gold or white and release a gentle, fragrant aroma. 20-30 seconds is the perfect window. At 15 seconds, the garlic won't be flavorful enough. At 60 seconds, it starts to brown. I often remove the pan from heat immediately after the shrimp finishes and add the garlic, then count to 25 slowly. This gives me a consistent result every time. 20-30 seconds Garlic becomes fragrant and slightly softened, no browning visible, aroma shifts from harsh to mellow and sweet
  7. 7 Remove the pan from heat immediately. This is perhaps the most important sentence in these instructions. Remove the pan. From the heat. Now. The residual heat in that pan is still high enough to continue cooking the shrimp through carryover heat—the temperature won't immediately drop just because you've removed it from the flame. The pan remains hot for several minutes. If the shrimp sit in this hot pan for even 30 seconds longer, the proteins continue tightening and squeeze out moisture. Your perfect shrimp becomes noticeably tougher. Squeeze the lemon wedges over the shrimp and garlic. The fresh lemon juice brightens the shrimp's natural brininess and sweetness, preventing the dish from tasting flat or one-dimensional. If using the optional butter, add it now. The residual heat will melt the butter into the oil and lemon juice, creating a light sauce that's silky and luxurious without overwhelming the delicate shrimp flavor. Toss everything together very gently for 10-15 seconds to combine. Gentle is the operative word—rough handling can break apart the just-cooked shrimp. 1 minute Lemon juice distributed throughout pan, butter (if using) melted and incorporated, aroma becomes bright and citrus-forward, shrimp glisten from oil and lemon
  8. 8 Transfer the shrimp immediately to a serving plate or bowl. Speed matters here. Warm plates are better than room-temperature plates because they slow the cooling process, keeping the shrimp at optimal serving temperature longer. If serving to guests, warm the plates by running hot water over them while cooking, then dry them thoroughly. Arrange the shrimp on the plate immediately. Serving shrimp family-style in a shared bowl works beautifully and feels generous. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley if desired. The green color adds visual interest and the parsley's fresh flavor complements the shrimp and lemon. Taste a piece and assess the seasoning. The combination of lemon and the natural brininess of shrimp usually requires minimal additional salt—I rarely add more than a pinch. If the shrimp tastes flat or one-dimensional despite the lemon, add a light sprinkle of fleur de sel or Maldon salt (which has a different crystal structure and dissolves on the tongue rather than making the shrimp taste salted). The dish tastes best served immediately while the shrimp is still warm and the pan sauce is still fluid. Once it cools, the oil solidifies slightly and the texture becomes less appealing. 1 minute Shrimp transferred to warm serving plate, still steaming slightly, pan sauce coating the pieces, parsley visible as garnish, shrimp glistens from oil and lemon

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:title: Pan-Seared Shrimp with Garlic and Lemon: Technique and Timing
media:content.media:description: A straightforward guide to cooking shrimp perfectly every time. Learn why proper pan searing requires dry shrimp, high heat, and minimal crowding, plus the exact doneness cues that prevent overcooking this delicate protein.

media:group.media:keywords: cat1761230072178, Easypost, quickpost, 30minutemeals, post, cooking, foodie, homemade, delicious, large, extravirgin, cloves
